title: pithecoctenium callistegioides
intro: Pithecoctenium callistegioides, also known as the "beach morning glory," is a species of flowering plant in the Convolvulaceae family that is native to tropical regions of the Americas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Pithecoctenium callistegioides include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as serving as an antivenom for snake bites. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures for topical and internal use, although its uses and preparations may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
